g.text: GKL — Fantasy Baseball Command Center
Ask Skipper
Model:
Opus 4.6
| F2 to change
Skipper here. Ask me anything about your league — standings, matchups, rosters, free agents.
You: Hey, Skipper! Give a season to date rundown of each team's performance, strengths, and weaknesses through the current week of this year's fantasy season.
Skipper: Alright, I've got the full picture. Here's your **Season-to-Date League Rundown** through Week 4 of the 2026 GKL season:
# 🏆 GKL 2026 — Season-to-Date Team Breakdown
## TIER 1: THE FRONTRUNNERS
### 1. Boys of Summer (Ryan) — 🥇 Roto: 1st (241 pts) | H2H: 3-0-1
**The complete package.** Ryan's squad is the league's best team by virtually every measure. They lead or rank near the top in R (122), H (181), HR (30), AVG
(.278), OBP (.363), SLG (.486), and they're getting it done on the mound too — strong ERA (3.53), best WHIP in the league (1.16), and 9 QS. Their power ranking
record of 58-6-4 is absurd — they'd beat almost everyone almost every week.
- **Strengths:** Elite offense across the board; pitching ratios are rock solid; great QS production
- **Weaknesses:** Middle-of-the-pack in APP (49), K (142), and HLD (4). Could use more pitching volume.
- **Schedule note:** Faced a moderately tough schedule (avg opp roto: 190.6) and still dominated. Upcoming matchups vs. Holy Toledo, The Revs, and Steel City are
very manageable.
### 2. My Name is My Name (Jonathan) — Roto: 2nd (219 pts) | H2H: 2-2-0
**Pitching-first powerhouse that's underperforming in H2H.** Jonathan's squad is 2nd in roto but only 2-2 in matchups — a product of two close losses in Weeks 3-4.
His pitching staff is elite: league-leading 12 W (tied), 167 K, strong ERA (3.01), and a 2.74 K/BB. His offense is solid in OBP (.346) and RBI (82) but light on
power (18 HR) and speed (11 SB).
- **Strengths:** Dominant pitching across nearly every category; high OBP lineup
- **Weaknesses:** Below-average HR, SB, and SLG. The offense needs more pop.
- **Schedule note:** Power record of 39-26-3 says he's a true top-4 team. Had an easy early schedule but drops in Weeks 3-4 came against mid-tier opponents. Tougher
road ahead.
### 3. Kirby Puckett (Scooter) — Roto: 3rd (217 pts) | H2H: 2-2-0
**The triples king with sneaky-good pitching.** Scooter leads the league with 8 3B — a massive advantage in that scarce category. He also tops the league with 14 W
and 12 QS, giving him elite starter production. ERA (3.65) and WHIP (1.21) are respectable, and K/BB (3.00) is strong.
- **Strengths:** League-best W and QS; dominant 3B; solid K/BB and pitching volume
- **Weaknesses:** Only 12 HR and .394 SLG — the offense seriously lacks power. Just 65 RBI is near the bottom. Week 4 was a rough 3-14 loss to Steel City.
- **Schedule note:** 2-2 is a fair reflection — power record of 37-28-3 is good but not elite. Has a favorable stretch coming (Y&B, ShapeShifters, Tenacious D).
### 4. What Can Braun Do 4U (Matthew) — Roto: 4th (213 pts) | H2H: 2-1-1
**Elite ratios, stingy pitching, but needs more volume.** Matthew's staff leads the league in ERA (2.81) and his K/BB (3.10) is excellent. He's got 9 SV and 9 HLD
for nice relief production. Offensively, the .258 AVG, .343 OBP, and .431 SLG are all upper-tier. But this team's power record (43-20-5) screams that 2-1-1 doesn't
do them justice — they've had the **easiest schedule in the league** (avg opp roto: 140.6).
- **Strengths:** Best ERA in the league; balanced relief contributions (SV+HLD); strong OBP and SLG
- **Weaknesses:** Low APP (66 sounds high, but only 3 QS — volume starters aren't giving length), just 7 SB. Tough schedule ahead could be a reality check.
- **Schedule note:** Has faced the weakest opponents so far. Upcoming: Comeback Season, My Name is My Name, Tybee Island. The difficulty ramps up considerably.
Ask Skipper a question...
esc
Back
f2
Model (F2)
^p
palette
